channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Black Hat USA
Bank Trojan 'Casbaneiro' Worms Through Latin America
item.description: Augmented Marauder's multipronged banking-Trojan cyber campaigns are targeting Spanish speakers, evading detection, and replicating rapidly.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Researchers Uncover Mining Operation Using ISO Lures to Spread RATs and Crypto Miners
item.description: A&nbsp;financially motivated operation&nbsp;codenamed REF1695&nbsp;has been&nbsp;observed leveraging fake installers to deploy remote access trojans (RATs) and cryptocurrency miners since November&nbsp;2023. "Beyond cryptomining, the threat actor monetizes infections through CPA (Cost Per Action) fraud, directing victims to content locker pages under the guise of software registration," Elastic
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The State of Trusted Open Source Report
item.description: In December&nbsp;2025, we shared the first-ever The State of Trusted Open&nbsp;Source report, featuring insights from our product data and customer base on open source consumption across our catalog of container image projects, versions, images, language libraries, and builds. These&nbsp;insights shed light on what teams pull, deploy, and maintain day to day, alongside the vulnerabilities and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Possible US Government iPhone Hacking Tool Leaked
item.description: <p>Wired <a href="https://www.wired.com/story/coruna-iphone-hacking-toolkit-us-government/">writes</a> (alternate <a href="https://archive.ph/vN8U5">source</a>):</p> <blockquote><p>Security researchers at Google on Tuesday <a href="https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/coruna-powerful-ios-exploit-kit">released a report</a> describing what they&#8217;re calling &#8220;Coruna,&#8221; a highly sophisticated iPhone hacking toolkit that includes five complete hacking techniques capable of bypassing all the defenses of an iPhone to silently install malware on a device when it visits a website containing the exploitation code. In total, Coruna takes advantage of 23 distinct vulnerabilities in iOS, a rare collection of hacking components that suggests it was created by a well-resourced, likely state-sponsored group of hackers...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: WhatsApp Alerts 200 Users After Fake iOS App Installed Spyware; Italian Firm Faces Action
item.description: Meta-owned messaging platform WhatsApp said it alerted about 200 users who were tricked into installing a bogus version of its iOS app that was infected with&nbsp;spyware. According to reports from Italian&nbsp;newspaper La Repubblica and news&nbsp;agency ANSA, the vast majority of the targets are located in Italy. It's assessed that the threat actors behind the activity used social engineering
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most CNI Firms Face Up to £5m in Downtime from OT Attacks
item.description: E2e-assure says 80% of critical infrastructure providers could face millions in downtime from cyber-attacks

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18.7.7 Update to More Devices to Block DarkSword Exploit
item.description: Apple&nbsp;on&nbsp;Wednesday expanded the availability of iOS 18.7.7&nbsp;and iPadOS 18.7.7&nbsp;to a broader range of devices to protect users from the risk posed by a recently disclosed exploit kit known&nbsp;as DarkSword. "We enabled the availability of iOS 18.7.7&nbsp;for more devices on April 1, 2026, so users with Automatic Updates turned on can automatically receive important security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ransomware Will Hit Hospitals. Rehearsals Are Key to Defense
item.description: A chief medical information officer provided a peek into what hospitals face when they inevitably suffer a ransomware attack—whether it leads to short or long-term outages.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: LatAm's Self-Taught Cyber Talent Overlooked Amid Cyberattack Glut
item.description: A newly released study exclusively shared with Dark Reading details the unique circumstances that make up Latin America's labor pool, and why organizations may want to expand their talent search.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Is “Hackback” Official US Cybersecurity Strategy?
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Cyberattacks Intensify Pressure on Latin American Governments
item.description: Cyber threats across Latin America are increasingly targeting government systems, from disruptive attacks in Puerto Rico to a surge of probes against Colombia’s health sector.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: CERT-UA Impersonation Campaign Spread AGEWHEEZE Malware to 1 Million Emails
item.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has disclosed details of a new phishing campaign in which the cybersecurity agency itself was impersonated to distribute a remote administration tool known as AGEWHEEZE. As part of the attacks, the threat actors, tracked as UAC-0255, sent emails on March 26 and 27, 2026, posing as CERT-UA to distribute a password-protected ZIP archive
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: A Panel Discussion on Cryptography
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Microsoft: Hackers Are Using WhatsApp to Deliver Malware to Windows PCs
item.description: <p>Hackers are using WhatsApp messages to deliver malware to Windows PCs, exploiting user trust and attachments to trigger stealthy, multi-stage attacks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-whatsapp-malware-windows-attack/">Microsoft: Hackers Are Using WhatsApp to Deliver Malware to Windows PCs</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Google Introduces Android Dev Verification Amid Openness Debate
item.description: Android requires dev identity verification for sideloaded apps; phased global rollout from September

item.title: Venom Stealer MaaS Platform Commoditizes ClickFix Attacks
item.description: A new service on the cybercrime market provides automated capabilities to create persistent information-stealing social engineering attacks.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Microsoft Warns of WhatsApp-Delivered VBS Malware Hijacking Windows via UAC Bypass
item.description: Microsoft is calling attention to a new campaign that has leveraged WhatsApp messages to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBS) files. The activity, beginning in late February 2026, leverages these scripts to initiate a multi-stage infection chain for establishing persistence and enabling remote access. It's currently not known what lures the threat actors use to trick users into
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Warns Quantum Computers Could Crack Crypto Sooner Than Expected
item.description: <p>Google warns that quantum computers could break crypto sooner than expected, heightening the urgency for post-quantum security across blockchain networks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-quantum-computing-crypto-security-risk/">Google Warns Quantum Computers Could Crack Crypto Sooner Than Expected</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Madeline Clarke

item.title: New Venom Stealer MaaS Platform Automates Continuous Data Theft
item.description: Venom Stealer malware-as-a-service automates ClickFix social engineering, credential and crypto exfiltration

item.title: Block the Prompt, Not the Work: The End of "Doctor No"
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Casbaneiro Phishing Targets Latin America and Europe Using Dynamic PDF Lures
item.description: A multi-pronged phishing campaign is targeting Spanish-speaking users in organizations across Latin America and Europe to deliver Windows banking trojans like Casbaneiro (aka Metamorfo) via another malware called Horabot. The activity has been attributed to a Brazilian cybercrime threat actor tracked as Augmented Marauder and Water Saci. The e-crime group was first documented by Trend Micro in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target European Governments in Espionage Campaigns
item.description: Chinese state-backed group TA416 had suspended its cyber espionage operations in Europe since 2023, noted Proofpoint

item.title: New Chrome Zero-Day CVE-2026-5281 Under Active Exploitation — Patch Released
item.description: Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address 21 vulnerabilities, including a zero-day flaw that it said has been exploited in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-5281 (CVSS score: N/A), concerns a use-after-free bug in Dawn, an open-source and cross-platform implementation of the WebGPU standard. "Use-after-free in Dawn in Google Chrome prior
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 3 Reasons Attackers Are Using Your Trusted Tools Against You (And Why You Don’t See It Coming)
item.description: For years, cybersecurity has followed a familiar model: block malware, stop the attack. Now, attackers are moving on to what’s next. Threat actors now use malware less frequently in favor of what’s already inside your environment, including abusing trusted tools, native binaries, and legitimate admin utilities to move laterally, escalate privileges, and persist without raising alarms. Most
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Are We Training AI Too Late?
item.description: Ask the Expert: Cybersecurity teams need to expand their field of view to include new, unique threat sources, rather than relying on past, proven threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Nishawn Smagh

item.title: A Taxonomy of Cognitive Security
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Eight in 10 UK Manufacturers Hit by Cyber Incident in a Year
item.description: Most UK manufacturers compromised last year suffered financial loss, says ESET

item.title: Digital assets after death: Managing risks to your loved one’s digital estate
item.description: Fraudsters often target the accounts of the deceased or their grieving relatives. Here’s how to keep the scammers at bay.

item.title: Hackers Hijack Axios npm Package to Spread RATs
item.description: Threat actors hijacked the popular npm package axios to spread RAT malware after compromising an open‑source maintainer’s account, researchers warn

item.title: Google Attributes Axios npm Supply Chain Attack to North Korean Group UNC1069
item.description: Google has formally attributed the supply chain compromise of the popular Axios npm package to a financially motivated North Korean threat activity cluster tracked as UNC1069. "We have attributed the attack to a suspected North Korean threat actor we track as UNC1069," John Hultquist, chief analyst at Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), told The Hacker News in a statement. "North Korean
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Claude Code Source Leaked via npm Packaging Error, Anthropic Confirms
item.description: Anthropic on Tuesday confirmed that internal code for its popular artificial intelligence (AI) coding assistant, Claude Code, had been inadvertently released due to a human error. "No sensitive customer data or credentials were involved or exposed," an Anthropic spokesperson said in a statement shared with CNBC News. "This was a release packaging issue caused by human error, not a security
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Forgotten Endpoint: Security Risks of Dormant Devices
item.description: Technology Talk: That forgotten notebook holds plenty of secrets to enterprise access.
item.dc:creator: Abib Oyebamiji

item.title: New North Korean AI Hiring Scheme Targets US Companies
item.description: <p>North Korean operatives are using AI-generated resumes and stolen identities to infiltrate US companies, turning hiring pipelines into a new attack vector.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-north-korean-ai-hiring-scheme-us-companies/">New North Korean AI Hiring Scheme Targets US Companies</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Axios NPM Package Compromised in Precision Attack
item.description: The NPM package for Axios, a popular JavaScript HTTP client library, was briefly compromised this week, possibly by North Korean threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Google's Vertex AI Is Over-Privileged. That's a Problem
item.description: Palo Alto Networks researchers show how attackers could exploit AI agents on Google's Vertex AI to steal data and break into restricted cloud infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: TeamPCP Breaches Cloud, SaaS Instances With Stolen Credentials
item.description: The threat group's shift to speedy attacks on AWS, Azure, and SaaS instances shows organizations need to respond quickly to compromised credentials.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Android Developer Verification Rollout Begins Ahead of September Enforcement
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Drive Expands AI Ransomware Detection, File Recovery to More Users
item.description: <p>Google expands Drive ransomware detection and file recovery with its latest AI model, which detects 14 times more infections as the features move beyond beta.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-drive-ransomware-detection-file-recovery/">Google Drive Expands AI Ransomware Detection, File Recovery to More Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks on Southeast Asian Government Networks
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in the TrueConf client video conferencing software has been exploited in the wild as a zero-day as part of a campaign targeting government entities in Southeast Asia dubbed TrueChaos. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-3502 (CVSS score: 7.8), a lack of integrity check when fetching application update code, allowing an attacker to distribute a tampered update,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Maryland Man Charged Over $53m Uranium Finance Crypto Hack
item.description: Maryland man accused of $53m Uranium Finance hack, exploited smart contract flaws, laundered funds

item.title: AI and Quantum Are Forcing a Rethink of Digital Trust
item.description: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, DigiCert CEO Amit Sinha explains how AI-driven identities and quantum threats are reshaping the foundations of digital trust.
item.dc:creator: Terry Sweeney

item.title: Phantom Project Bundles Infostealer, Crypter and RAT For Sale
item.description: Phantom Stealer .NET harvests browser credentials, cookies, cards, sessions, as stealer-as-a-service

item.title: Iran Deploys 'Pseudo-Ransomware,' Revives Pay2Key Operations
item.description: Iranian APTs are blurring the lines between state-sponsored and cybercriminal activities to target high-impact US organizations.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Vertex AI Vulnerability Exposes Google Cloud Data and Private Artifacts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security "blind spot" in Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform that could allow artificial intelligence (AI) agents to be weaponized by an attacker to gain unauthorized access to sensitive data and compromise an organization's cloud environment. According to Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, the issue relates to how the Vertex AI permission model can be misused
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ChatGPT Security Issue Enabled Data Theft via Single Prompt
item.description: OpenAI has patched vulnerability, which Check Point said was because of a DNS loophole

item.title: TeamPCP Explores Ways to Exploit Stolen Supply Chain Secrets
item.description: TeamPCP is exploring ways to monetize the secrets harvested during supply chain attacks, with identified ties to the Lapsus$ and Vect ransomware gangs

item.title: The AI Arms Race – Why Unified Exposure Management Is Becoming a Boardroom Priority
item.description: The cybersecurity landscape is accelerating at an unprecedented rate. What is emerging is not simply a rise in the number of vulnerabilities or tools, but a dramatic increase in speed. Speed of attack, speed of exploitation, and speed of change across modern environments. This is the defining challenge of the new era of digital warfare: the weaponization of Artificial Intelligence. Threat actors
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Silver Fox Expands Asia Cyber Campaign with AtlasCross RAT and Fake Domains
item.description: Chinese-speaking users are the target of an active campaign that uses typosquatted domains impersonating trusted software brands to deliver a previously undocumented remote access trojan named AtlasCross RAT. "The operation covers VPN clients, encrypted messengers, video conferencing tools, cryptocurrency trackers, and e-commerce applications, with eleven confirmed delivery domains impersonating
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Inventors of Quantum Cryptography Win Turing Award
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Employee Data Breaches Surge to Seven-Year High
item.description: Analysis from law firm Nockolds suggests non-cyber incidents are driving up employee data breaches

item.title: NCSC Urges Immediate Patching of F5 BIG-IP Bug
item.description: The National Cyber Security Centre wants UK firms to patch CVE-2025-53521

item.title: This month in security with Tony Anscombe – March 2026 edition
item.description: The past four weeks have seen a slew of new cybersecurity wake-up calls that showed why every organization needs a well-thought-out cyber-resilience plan

item.title: Axios Supply Chain Attack Pushes Cross-Platform RAT via Compromised npm Account
item.description: The popular HTTP client known as Axios has suffered a supply chain attack after two newly published versions of the npm package introduced a malicious dependency that delivers a trojan capable of targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. Versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 of Axios have been found to inject "plain-crypto-js" version 4.2.1 as a fake dependency. According to StepSecurity, the two
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI-Powered 'DeepLoad' Malware Steals Credentials, Evades Detection
item.description: The massive amount of junk code that hides the malware's logic from security scans was almost certainly generated by AI, researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: AI-Driven Code Surge Is Forcing a Rethink of AppSec
item.description: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, Black Duck CEO Jason Schmitt explains how AI is reshaping application security and why it must evolve to keep pace.
item.dc:creator: Terry Sweeney

item.title: F5 BIG-IP Vulnerability Reclassified as RCE, Under Exploitation
item.description: CVE-2025-53521 was initially disclosed in October as a high-severity denial-of-service (DoS) flaw, but new information has revealed the bug is actually much more dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: OpenAI Patches ChatGPT Data Exfiltration Flaw and Codex GitHub Token Vulnerability
item.description: A previously unknown vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT allowed sensitive conversation data to be exfiltrated without user knowledge or consent, according to new findings from Check Point. "A single malicious prompt could turn an otherwise ordinary conversation into a covert exfiltration channel, leaking user messages, uploaded files, and other sensitive content," the cybersecurity company said in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Flywire
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Manufacturing &amp; Healthcare Share Struggles with Passwords
item.description: The two key economic sectors struggle with security for a reason: Many insiders view access management as a roadblock, while attackers see it as a way in.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: DeepLoad Malware Uses ClickFix and WMI Persistence to Steal Browser Credentials
item.description: A new campaign has leveraged the ClickFix social engineering tactic as a way to distribute a previously undocumented malware loader referred to as DeepLoad. "It likely uses AI-assisted obfuscation and process injection to evade static scanning, while credential theft starts immediately and captures passwords and sessions even if the primary loader is blocked," ReliaQuest researchers Thassanai
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Storm Brews Over Critical, No-Click Telegram Flaw
item.description: The vulnerability, which is allegedly triggered by a corrupted sticker in the messaging app, received a 9.8 CVSS score, but Telegram denies it exists.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Cybercriminals Exploit Tax Season With New Phishing Tactics
item.description: Tax-season phishing floods deliver RMM malware, credential theft, BEC and tax-form scams

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director Kash Patel’s Email, Leak Messages Online
item.description: <p>Iran-linked hackers breached FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email, leaking older messages in a retaliatory cyberattack with no classified data exposed.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-iran-linked-hackers-breach-kash-patel/">Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director Kash Patel’s Email, Leak Messages Online</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Lloyds IT Glitch Exposed Data of Nearly 500,000 Banking Customers
item.description: Lloyds app glitch exposed up to 447,936 customers’ transactions and personal data during update

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Telecom Sleeper Cells, LLM Jailbreaks, Apple Forces U.K. Age Checks and More
item.description: Some weeks are loud. This one was quieter but not in a good way. Long-running operations are finally hitting courtrooms, old attack methods are showing up in new places, and research that stopped being theoretical right around the time defenders stopped paying attention. There's a bit of everything this week. Persistence plays, legal wins, influence ops, and at least one thing that looks boring
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 3 SOC Process Fixes That Unlock Tier 1 Productivity
item.description: What is really slowing Tier 1 down: the threat itself or the process around it? In many SOCs, the biggest delays do not come from the threat alone. They come from fragmented workflows, manual triage steps, and limited visibility early in the investigation. Fixing those process gaps can help Tier 1 move faster, reduce unnecessary escalations, and improve how the entire SOC responds under pressure
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Russian CTRL Toolkit Delivered via Malicious LNK Files Hijacks RDP via FRP Tunnels
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a remote access toolkit of Russian-origin that's distributed via malicious Windows shortcut (LNK) files that are disguised as private key folders. The CTRL toolkit, according to Censys, is custom-built using .NET and includes various executables" to facilitate credential phishing, keylogging, Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) hijacking, and reverse tunneling
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DeepLoad Malware Combines ClickFix With AI-Generated Code to Avoid Detection
item.description: Researchers at ReliaQuest warn of persistent malware campaign targeting enterprise credentials

item.title: The State of Secrets Sprawl 2026: 9 Takeaways for CISOs
item.description: Secrets sprawl isn't slowing down: in 2025, it accelerated faster than most security teams anticipated. GitGuardian's State of Secrets Sprawl 2026 report analyzed billions of commits across public GitHub and uncovered 29 million new hardcoded secrets in 2025 alone, a 34% increase year over year and the largest single-year jump ever recorded. This year's findings reveal three core trends: AI has
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple’s Camera Indicator Lights
item.description: <p>A <a href="https://daringfireball.net/2026/03/apple_enclaves_neo_camera_indicator">thoughtful review</a> of Apple&#8217;s system to alert users that the camera is on. It&#8217;s really well-designed, and important in a world where malware could surreptitiously start recording.</p> <blockquote><p>The reason it&#8217;s tempting to think that a dedicated camera indicator light is more secure than an on-display indicator is the fact that hardware is generally more secure than software, because it&#8217;s harder to tamper with. With hardware, a dedicated hardware indicator light can be connected to the camera hardware such that if the camera is accessed, the light must turn on, with no way for software running on the device, no matter its privileges, to change that. With an indicator light that is rendered on the display, it&#8217;s not foolish to worry that malicious software, with sufficient privileges, could draw over the pixels on the display where the camera indicator is rendered, disguising that the camera is in use...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Critical Citrix NetScaler Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: Researchers from watchTowr and Defused have found evidence that attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-3055, a critical NetScaler vulnerability

item.title: ICO Fines UK Nuisance Call Scammers £100,000
item.description: The UK Information Commissioner’s Office has handed a £100,000 fine to Birmingham-based TMAC

item.title: European Commission Confirms Cloud Data Breach
item.description: The European Commission has revealed details of a data breach impacting its AWS infrastructure

item.title: Three China-Linked Clusters Target Southeast Asian Government in 2025 Cyber Campaign
item.description: Three threat activity clusters aligned with China have targeted a government organization in Southeast Asia as part of what has been described as a "complex and well-resourced operation." The campaigns have led to the deployment of various malware families, including HIUPAN (aka USBFect, MISTCLOAK, or U2DiskWatch), PUBLOAD, EggStremeFuel (aka RawCookie), EggStremeLoader (aka Gorem RAT), MASOL
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Shadow AI: Managing the Security Risks of Unsanctioned AI Tools | UpGuard
item.description: Uncover the hidden risks of unapproved AI tools in your organization. Learn to detect, manage, and secure sensitive data from shadow AI threats.

item.title: The Shadow AI Data Leak Problem No One's Talking About | UpGuard
item.description: Is your team unknowingly leaking sensitive data through its AI usage? Discover the dangers of Shadow AI and strategies for secure AI adoption at work.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director’s Personal Email, Hit Stryker With Wiper Attack
item.description: Threat actors with ties to Iran successfully broke into the personal email account of Kash Patel, the director of the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), and leaked a cache of photos and other documents to the internet. Handala Hack Team, which carried out the breach, said on its website that Patel "will now find his name among the list of successfully hacked victims." In a statement
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Citrix NetScaler Under Active Recon for CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) Memory Overread Bug
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway is witnessing active reconnaissance activity, according to Defused Cyber and watchTowr. The vulnerability, CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS score: 9.3), refers to a case of insufficient input validation leading to memory overread, which an attacker could exploit to leak potentially sensitive information. Per
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: TA446 Deploys DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit in Targeted Spear-Phishing Campaign
item.description: Proofpoint has disclosed details of a targeted email campaign in which threat actors with ties to Russia are leveraging the recently disclosed DarkSword exploit kit to target iOS devices. The activity has been attributed with high confidence to the Russian state-sponsored threat group known as TA446, which is also tracked by the broader cybersecurity community under the monikers Callisto,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds CVE-2025-53521 to KEV After Active F5 BIG-IP APM Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a critical security flaw impacting F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager (APM) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-53521 (CVSS v4 score: 9.3), which could allow a threat actor to achieve remote code execution. "When a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Bioluminescent Bacteria in Squid
item.description: <p>The Hawaiian bobtail squid has <a href="https://phys.org/news/2026-03-bioluminescent-bacterial-partner-essential-squid.html">bioluminescent bacteria</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Apple Sends Lock Screen Alerts to Outdated iPhones Over Active Web-Based Exploits
item.description: Apple is now sending Lock Screen notifications to iPhones and iPads running older versions of iOS and iPadOS to alert users of web-based attacks and urge them to install the update. The development was first reported by MacRumors. "Apple is aware of attacks targeting out-of-date iOS software, including the version on your iPhone. Install this critical update to protect your iPhone," the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: TeamPCP Pushes Malicious Telnyx Versions to PyPI, Hides Stealer in WAV Files
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the supply chain attack targeting Trivy, KICS, and litellm, has now compromised the telnyx Python package by pushing two malicious versions to steal sensitive data. The two versions, 4.87.1 and 4.87.2, published to the Python Package Index (PyPI) repository on March 27, 2026, concealed their credential harvesting capabilities within a .WAV file. Users are
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China Upgrades the Backdoor It Uses to Spy on Telcos Globally
item.description: Chinese APT Red Menshen's super-advanced BPFdoor malware defeats traditional cybersecurity protections. All telcos can do, really, is try hunting it down.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Wartime Usage of Compromised IP Cameras Highlight Their Danger
item.description: The list of countries exploiting Internet-connected cameras to give them eyes inside their adversaries' borders continues to expand. What should companies look out for?
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: New Wave of AiTM Phishing Targets TikTok for Business
item.description: Push Security has uncovered a new AiTM phishing campaign targeting TikTok for Business accounts using Google and TikTok themed login pages

item.title: TeamPCP Targets Telnyx Package in Latest PyPI Software Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Socket and Endor Labs discovered a new TeamPCP campaign leading to the delivery of credential-stealing malware

item.title: AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Industry Shifts Define This Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from March 23–27.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-upgrades-security-breaches-and-industry-shifts-define-this-week-in-tech/">AI Upgrades, Security Breaches, and Industry Shifts Define This Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Open VSX Bug Let Malicious VS Code Extensions Bypass Pre-Publish Security Checks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched bug impacting Open VSX's pre-publish scanning pipeline to cause the tool to allow a malicious Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension to pass the vetting process and go live in the registry. "The pipeline had a single boolean return value that meant both 'no scanners are configured' and 'all scanners failed to run,'" Koi
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Infrastructure Attacks With Physical Consequences Down 25%
item.description: Operational technology (OT) at industrial and critical infrastructure sites seem to have been benefitting from a lull in ransomware, and hackers' relative ignorance of OT systems.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Google Issues High-Risk Security Patch for 3.5 Billion Chrome Users: What You Need to Know
item.description: <p>Google patches eight high-severity Chrome vulnerabilities affecting 3.5 billion users. Here’s why you should update and relaunch your browser now.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chrome-update-8-vulnerabilities-3-5-billion-users/">Google Issues High-Risk Security Patch for 3.5 Billion Chrome Users: What You Need to Know</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Google Sets 2029 Deadline for Quantum-Safe Cryptography
item.description: The post-quantum future may be coming sooner than you think, as Google plans to have PQC migration in place by 2029.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Quantum Computing Threat to Encryption Is Closer Than Expected, Warns Google
item.description: ‘Q-Day’ and the cybersecurity problems it brings could come as early as 2029 as Google accelerates its post-quantum cryptography migration

item.title: AitM Phishing Targets TikTok Business Accounts Using Cloudflare Turnstile Evasion
item.description: Threat actors are using adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) phishing pages to seize control of TikTok for Business accounts in a new campaign, according to a report from Push Security. Business accounts associated with social media platforms are a lucrative target, as they can be weaponized by bad actors for malvertising and distributing malware. "TikTok has been historically abused to distribute
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Cracks Down on Chinese Crypto Marketplace for Funding Southeast Asia Scam Hubs
item.description: The UK government has sanctioned Xinbi, described as “the second-largest illicit online marketplace ever”

item.title: We Are At War
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: RSAC 2026 wrap-up – Week in security with Tony Anscombe
item.description: This year, AI agents took the center stage – as a defensive capability, but more pressingly as a risk many organizations haven't caught up with

item.title: Bearlyfy Hits Russian Firms with Custom GenieLocker Ransomware
item.description: A pro-Ukrainian group called Bearlyfy has been attributed to more than 70 cyber attacks targeting Russian companies since it first surfaced in the threat landscape in January 2025, with recent attacks leveraging a custom Windows ransomware strain codenamed GenieLocker. "Bearlyfy (also known as Labubu) operates as a dual-purpose group aimed at inflicting maximum damage upon Russian businesses;
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LangChain, LangGraph Flaws Expose Files, Secrets, Databases in Widely Used AI Frameworks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed three security vulnerabilities impacting LangChain and LangGraph that, if successfully exploited, could expose filesystem data, environment secrets, and conversation history. Both LangChain and LangGraph are open-source frameworks that are used to build applications powered by Large Language Models (LLMs). LangGraph is built on the foundations of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
